stocks.name: ARM
stocks.explanation: Arm Holdings surged after Nvidia unveiled its RTX Spark Arm-based PC chip at Computex, validating Arm architecture for mainstream Windows laptops. Multiple analysts raised price targets simultaneously, with Mizuho going to $425 and Wells Fargo to $410. The broader semiconductor rally added further momentum.

stocks.name: MDB
stocks.explanation: MongoDB jumped after reporting Q1 fiscal 2027 revenue of $687.6 million, up 25% year-over-year, beating estimates. Management raised full-year guidance to $2.92-$2.96 billion, citing accelerating momentum in Atlas platform adoption and AI-related database deployments. Multiple analysts raised price targets.

stocks.name: HUBS
stocks.explanation: HubSpot rallied after presenting at the Jefferies Software, Internet, and AI Conference, where management reinforced Q1 execution strength and AI-led product strategy. The move was amplified by a broad enterprise software rally triggered by Snowflake's blowout earnings and Dell's AI-driven results.

stocks.name: ZS
stocks.explanation: Zscaler climbed following strong fiscal Q3 results showing 25% revenue growth to $850.5 million with ARR reaching $3.53 billion. B.Riley upgraded the stock to Buy, arguing that enterprise AI tool adoption will drive increased demand for Zscaler's zero-trust security products. KeyBanc also raised its target to $190.

stocks.name: DDOG
stocks.explanation: Datadog extended gains as the observability leader benefited from a two-day AI conviction wave across software stocks. Q1 revenue had already topped $1 billion with 32% year-over-year growth, and JPMorgan still sees 43% upside, noting that AI workloads are making cloud infrastructure dramatically more complex.

stocks.name: NOW
stocks.explanation: ServiceNow rose as part of a broad enterprise software and AI infrastructure rally following Snowflake's record earnings session and Dell's AI server blowout. Oracle and Palantir also lifted alongside ServiceNow as investors rotated into cloud and AI platform plays.

stocks.name: CRM
stocks.explanation: Salesforce gained in the broader enterprise software rally driven by AI optimism after Snowflake and Dell earnings. The stock had been range-bound between $178 and $194 but broke higher as investors repriced the AI opportunity across the SaaS sector.

stocks.name: SNOW
stocks.explanation: Snowflake surged 36% on the week after reporting blowout earnings that sparked the broadest enterprise software rally in months. The company's AI and data cloud platform saw accelerating adoption, with results lifting the entire cloud software sector including ServiceNow, Oracle, and Workday.

stocks.name: VEEV
stocks.explanation: Veeva Systems climbed after announcing that Kindeva, a global CDMO, would adopt its Quality Cloud platform. The stock also benefited from the broad enterprise software rally, though it remains down 25% year-to-date and well below its 52-week high.

stocks.name: INTU
stocks.explanation: Intuit rebounded after a sharp 19% drop earlier in the week when it announced 17% workforce cuts under an AI restructuring plan and cut TurboTax guidance. The bounce reflected bargain hunting after multiple analysts maintained buy ratings despite reducing price targets.

stocks.name: WDAY
stocks.explanation: Workday rose as part of the enterprise software rally triggered by Snowflake's strong results and Dell's AI server momentum. The stock received a Buy rating driven by consistent earnings beats, growing return on total capital, and robust free cash flow generation.

stocks.name: TEAM
stocks.explanation: Atlassian jumped 11% in the enterprise software rally wave, benefiting from renewed AI optimism across developer and collaboration tools. GitLab climbed 7.5% and monday.com advanced 6% in the same broad sector rotation into software names.

stocks.name: NET
stocks.explanation: Cloudflare gained as part of the broad enterprise software and infrastructure rally. The company continues to benefit from growing demand for edge computing, security, and AI inference workloads as enterprises expand their cloud footprints.

stocks.name: SE
stocks.explanation: Sea Limited rallied as emerging market tech and e-commerce names gained alongside the broader software sector. The company's Shopee e-commerce platform and digital financial services continue to expand in Southeast Asia and Latin America.

stocks.name: ORCL
stocks.explanation: Oracle climbed after Oppenheimer raised its target to $235, citing expected upside from large AI and cloud bookings tied to OpenAI, Meta, Nvidia, and U.S. federal government contracts. The stock was further lifted by the enterprise software rally and a reported U.S. government cloud AI deal.
